model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The prior cosine-over-256 schedule with higher LR regressed; switching to AdamW with a slightly lower LR and stronger decoupled weight decay is a safer short-budget optimization dynamic for this compact CNN.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer settings are working well; a modest capacity increase in both convolutional width and classifier width should improve representational power without adding deep architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 80","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous width increase produced a strong gain, so the next low-complexity step is another moderate width expansion to test whether the fixed 256-step budget still benefits from added capacity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After two capacity increases, a slightly lower learning rate can improve stability and final validation loss within the same short fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With a short fixed-step budget, slightly reducing AdamW’s second-moment timescale can make adaptation more responsive and improve early optimization at the current learning rate.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Now that optimization settings are more stable, a cosine decay across the fixed training horizon may improve final-step generalization versus constant LR.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay substantially hurt this setup, so reverting to the stronger constant-learning-rate regime is the safest next step to recover the best-performing configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After increasing model capacity, slightly relaxing weight decay may reduce underfitting while preserving AdamW regularization in this short training budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 4e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Lowering weight decay regressed validation loss, so restoring the previous stronger regularization returns to the best-performing regime.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 80","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed step budget, a moderately larger batch can expose the model to more examples while keeping optimization behavior close to the current best setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 80","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The jump to batch 80 produced a large gain; testing a further moderate increase can improve data coverage per fixed step while staying close to the successful configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 112","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance keeps improving with larger batches under fixed steps, so the next incremental increase tests whether additional per-step data coverage continues to pay off.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 112","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Gains have remained monotonic as batch size increased, so testing 128 is a natural next step to further increase examples seen within the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 144","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Validation loss is still improving with larger batches, so increasing to 144 is a controlled next probe for more per-step data exposure without a drastic jump.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 144","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch 144 regressed, so reverting to batch 128 restores the strongest observed operating point before exploring nearby refinements.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 6e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With batch size now at 128, a modest learning-rate increase can better match the larger-batch optimization regime and may improve progress within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss is still improving with higher LR at batch 128, so one more incremental increase tests whether optimization remains under-driven at 7e-4 in the fixed-step regime.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Recent increments from 6e-4 to 7e-4 to 8e-4 each improved validation loss, so one final incremental LR increase is the highest-yield probe with limited remaining budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning-rate increases have consistently improved validation loss in the current high-batch regime, so using the final step to test 1e-3 is the strongest remaining scalar optimization probe.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}
